title: How the Fuel Subsidy Reform is Unlocking Nigeria's Economic Potential
excerpt: President Tinubu's bold decision to remove fuel subsidies has freed over ₦11 trillion annually, now being redirected to critical infrastructure and social investment.
content:
The removal of the fuel subsidy in May 2023 was one of the most consequential economic decisions in Nigeria's recent history. For decades, fuel subsidies consumed an ever-growing share of Nigeria's federal budget, leaving little room for investment in education, healthcare, and infrastructure.

What the Subsidy Was Costing Nigeria

The subsidy cost Nigeria over ₦11 trillion annually — money that could have built roads, schools, hospitals, and power infrastructure across all 36 states. President Tinubu made the difficult but necessary decision to end this drain on the national treasury.

Nigeria cannot subsidize consumption while neglecting investment in the future.

The freed funds are now being channeled into targeted welfare programs, infrastructure rehabilitation, and support for state governments through increased FAAC allocations.
author: RTIFN Media Team

title: RTIFN Osun Launches Ward-by-Ward Sensitization Campaign Across All 30 LGAs
excerpt: The Osun State chapter of RTIFN has begun an intensive grassroots mobilization drive to communicate President Tinubu's reform agenda to citizens at the ward level.
content:
RTIFN Osun State Chapter has commenced its most ambitious grassroots engagement drive to date, deploying trained volunteers to every ward across all 30 local government areas of Osun State.

Why Ward-Level Engagement Matters

Policy communication must reach citizens where they are — in their communities, markets, and homes. The RTIFN ward-level campaign brings simplified explanations of government reforms directly to the grassroots.

In the first phase, over 200 volunteers have been trained and deployed across the three senatorial districts of Osun State: Osun Central, Osun East, and Osun West.
author: Comrade Adebayo Taiwo

title: Record FAAC Allocations Under Tinubu: What It Means for Osun State
excerpt: Monthly federation account distributions to states have surged significantly under President Tinubu, giving Osun State and others more resources for local development.
content:
One of the less-discussed but highly impactful outcomes of President Tinubu's economic reforms is the dramatic increase in FAAC allocations to states and local governments across Nigeria.

Understanding FAAC

The Federation Account Allocation Committee (FAAC) distributes revenues collected by the federal government — from oil, taxes, and other sources — to the three tiers of government monthly. A higher FAAC allocation means more money for roads, schools, salaries, and development in Osun State.

Under the Tinubu administration, monthly FAAC distributions have grown substantially, directly benefiting all 30 LGAs of Osun State.
author: RTIFN Policy Desk

title: New Minimum Wage: Tinubu's Commitment to Nigerian Workers
excerpt: The approved new minimum wage signals the Tinubu administration's commitment to improving the welfare of workers across all sectors of the Nigerian economy.
content:
The Tinubu administration has made improving workers' welfare a central pillar of its economic reform agenda. The new minimum wage framework represents a significant step forward in the government's commitment to the Nigerian working class.

Key Details of the New Minimum Wage

The new minimum wage approved under President Tinubu's administration represents a meaningful increase in take-home pay for millions of Nigerian workers, particularly civil servants and low-income earners in both the public and private sectors.

The administration has also committed to reviewing the wage structure periodically to ensure it keeps pace with economic realities.
author: RTIFN Media Team

title: Press Release: RTIFN Osun Commends Federal Government on Infrastructure Investment
excerpt: RTIFN Osun State Chapter issues official statement commending the Federal Government's renewed commitment to road infrastructure and power sector investment.
content:
The Osun State Chapter of RTIFN (Relax, Tinubu Is Fixing Nigeria) wishes to commend the Federal Government of Nigeria under the leadership of President Bola Ahmed Tinubu for the renewed and vigorous investment in infrastructure across the nation.

We particularly note with satisfaction the ongoing road rehabilitation projects, the revival of the power sector reform agenda, and the commitment to completing long-abandoned federal projects in the South-West geopolitical zone.

Our Position

RTIFN Osun State Chapter calls on all citizens of Osun State to support these reforms, exercise patience as the economic transition matures, and join our movement in communicating the truth about Nigeria's transformation to their communities.

Signed,
The Executive Council
RTIFN Osun State Chapter
author: Office of the State Coordinator

title: How Cashless Government Operations Are Fighting Corruption
excerpt: The digitization of government payments under Tinubu's administration has significantly reduced leakages, ghost workers, and opacity in public financial management.
content:
One of the hallmarks of the Tinubu administration's approach to governance has been the aggressive digitization of government transactions and payment systems. This cashless approach is proving to be a powerful weapon against corruption and financial leakages.

Ghost Workers Eliminated

Through the implementation of the Integrated Personnel and Payroll Information System (IPPIS) and other digital payment platforms, thousands of ghost workers have been removed from government payrolls, saving billions of naira monthly.

Transparent Procurement

Government procurement processes have been moved online, reducing opportunities for kickbacks and ensuring more competitive pricing for public contracts.
author: RTIFN Policy Desk

title: RTIFN Osun Holds Successful Town Hall in Ife Central LGA
excerpt: Over 300 citizens attended RTIFN Osun's town hall meeting in Ife Central LGA, engaging directly with government policy explanations and reform updates.
content:
RTIFN Osun State Chapter held a highly successful town hall meeting in Ife Central Local Government Area, drawing an attendance of over 300 citizens from across the LGA's wards.

The event featured presentations on the Tinubu administration's economic reform agenda, interactive Q&A sessions, and the registration of new RTIFN members from the community.

Key Highlights

The State Coordinator, Engr. Kunle Oyediran, delivered the keynote address explaining the rationale behind the fuel subsidy removal and its long-term benefits for Nigeria. Community leaders, market women, and youth groups participated actively in the discussions.
author: RTIFN Media Team

title: Understanding Nigeria's Foreign Reserve Recovery Under Tinubu
excerpt: Nigeria's external reserves and foreign exchange market have shown significant improvement following the Tinubu administration's bold unification policy.
content:
The unification of Nigeria's foreign exchange market was one of the most immediate and bold economic decisions of the Tinubu administration. By eliminating the multiple exchange rate windows that had created arbitrage opportunities and discouraged foreign investment, the administration took a painful but necessary step toward economic stability.

Impact on Reserves

Nigeria's external reserves have shown a recovery trajectory as the forex unification policy has attracted diaspora remittances through official channels, reduced round-tripping by currency speculators, and improved investor confidence in the Nigerian economy.
author: RTIFN Policy Desk
